paragraphs.body: * The scene I wrote is not meant to happen immediately after the scene from my first submission. It’ll probably be somewhere in the middle of the novel.  The prologue is also (obviously) going to go at the very start of the novel. :) *
Short Prologue:

I am obsessive– this I know of myself. I am more obsessive than any other friend to come out of headquarters. It’s natural though, to be obsessed with our friends a little bit, right? We surely wouldn’t be friends with someone we hate. There’s nothing natural about me though, and there’s nothing natural about Marly and I’s friendship. Headquarters chose me for her, and the moment the buzzer went off, signaling the start of my journey, I knew my purpose. And my purpose is all I know.

annotations.text: I knew my purpose. And my purpose is all I know.
annotations.comment: The implication here, which is intriguing, is that she may learn a different or larger purpose in the book.

paragraphs.body: “Marly! Get your ass out here!” Becca screams from the living room.
“One second!” she replies, piling mascara onto her eyelashes.

Tonight is the “girls night” the girls have not shut up about. I always hate Marly when she does this. She gets drunk to cope with the emotions she doesn’t feel like dealing with and makes bad decisions, and I’m sure tonight will be no different.

annotations.comment: Hoo, boy, do I relate to this!

paragraphs.body: Marly finishes applying her mascara and slides her mini skirt over her legs, zipping it at her waist. She looks in the mirror at her reflection, and I can’t help but do the same. I hardly recognize her. I barely recognize myself. I’m weaker than ever, practically transparent, clinging to the ever so small memory of myself Marly stores in the depths of her mind. I don’t know how much longer I’ll be around, not unless I do something about it.

annotations.comment: I’d make a bit more of this — what each of them look like. Does Marly catch the eye of the narrator?

paragraphs.body: Just last night she needed me. She stormed into her room and threw her backpack against the wall as the tears flew down her face.
“ I failed!” she cried out, “ I literally failed the exam and I don’t know what to do.”
I don’t respond right away. She surely wouldn’t be talking to me. Marly hasn’t spoken to me in months. Not since she suspected Andre was lying to her. Even then she barely talked to me.
“ Please. I really don’t know what to do right now. I failed my exam for the first time ever and it literally feels like my life is falling apart. Help me.”
She was talking to me.

I can still recall the feeling being noticed again made me feel. The warm feeling that started towards the bottom of my body and spread upward. It filled me with more purpose than I’ve felt in years. I knew I couldn’t lose this again.

annotations.comment: Lovely. I wondered if she comes back to life/clarity a bit here?

paragraphs.body: “ Marly. It’s one exam. You’re a smart girl and it’s just one exam. I am here for you, and I will never leave.”

annotations.text: I am here for you, and I will never leave.”
annotations.comment: This felt a bit weird. Maybe the IF says it after a pause?

paragraphs.body: Marly doesn’t respond, but I know she is listening.
“I promise I can fix all of this. Just don’t leave.”

annotations.text: I can fix all of this
annotations.comment: Is that something the IF often says or is that a new thing? I’m intrigued by the idea that IF can manipulate the real world in some way, but I’m not sure that’s what’s being suggested here.

paragraphs.body: She didn’t answer and crawled into bed without changing into pajamas or taking off her makeup. I knew she was upset, but the tears didn’t fall for the rest of the night.
I felt so powerful that night, and knew I needed to feel like that forever.

annotations.comment: What is she going to do, though?

paragraphs.body: “I’m ready!” Marly exclaims as she opens her door, propping her left foot and shoulder blades on the door frame to pose for her roommates, “ I told you I’d only be a second!”
“ Oh girl, this was well worth the wait!” Sophia replies.

“As much as I would love to stand around and gawk at Marly until our eyes fall out of our face, but it's already 9:45 and we haven’t even gotten our night started…” says Becca as she grabs shot glasses from the cabinants.

annotations.comment: I wondered why everyone else cares about what Marly looks like so much, but perhaps that’s clear in the story before now.

paragraphs.body: Marly joins the girls around the small dining table and immediately shoots back a shot of tequila. They again begin to talk about how much they’ve been looking forward to a night out together like this since they decided they were going to live together last year. I tune most of this out, as it's nothing out of the ordinary, until I near Marly say something that wrecks me.
“ We’ve been needing a night of just us. Just Marly, Becca, and Sophia. No boys, no side characters. Just us”

Just us? Really? I shrivel at the idea of not being mentioned. I understand why she doesn’t mention me– I mean who at age nineteen would willingly admit to their friends that deep down inside they still care about their imaginary best friend even a tiny bit. I know it's not Becca and Sophia’s fault, anyone would respond with puzzled faces and awkward laughter, but I can’t help but blame them. I have been trying for years to make attempts to get back into Marly’s life, and now that I have an opening without Andre, they are closing it. I know now that I need to make one last attempt to get back into Marly’s life, no matter the risk.

annotations.text: anyone would respond with puzzled faces and awkward laughter,
annotations.comment: I was confused here. Again, this scene could use a little more to ignite the IF’s jealousy.

paragraphs.body: “Guys… I think we need to take another shot,” Becca stammers, an early sign she should already stop drinking.
“Yes!” the girls reply, filling their shot glasses to the brim.
This is the perfect time to initiate Phase One.
As the girls shoot their heads back, swallowing their shots, I knock the handle over, spilling it over the table.
“Dude?” Sophia says confusingly, “How did this even fall? Marly, Becca, did either of you just hit the bottle by accident?”
“I didn’t, I swear!” Becca replies.
“Me either,” Marly says, finally lifting the bottle, “I swear this keeps happening to me though…”
“What, you keep spilling tequila randomly?” Becca chirps, in a half serious, half joking tone.
“No. Like, like things keep happening around me without a cause. It’s weird…”
It’s not weird, Marly. It’s me… you have to know this.

annotations.text: y. It’s me… you have to know this.
annotations.comment: Yeah, I was confused here — doesn’t she guess this?

paragraphs.body: I try to listen to the girls discuss what ghost they think is haunting the place before I hear a faint voice speaking…
“ I know what you’re doing…” the voice whispers so quietly I can barely make it out.
No sooner than their final word, X appears from the depth of Sophia’s room. A shocking sight to see.

annotations.comment: Why and how is he shocking?

paragraphs.body: “ No, you don’t.”
“Yes I do. Trust me.”
Everything about X pisses me off. He has no idea who I am or what my plans are. I refuse to believe it.

“ And why should I trust you? I have no idea who you are, the only thing that we have in common is that we are friends. That’s it. We are not friends, just two friends who happen to have humans that live together.”

annotations.text: friends. That’s it. We are not friends, just two friends

paragraphs.body: “But we are the same. We’re both strung along, sad excuses of friends of 19 year-old girls who are too scared to grow up. I’ve been in your shoes before– I’ve wanted to break the rules. I got really close one time,” X exclaims as he approaches me, “The details don’t matter right now. But what I learned is that it was never going to be worth it.

annotations.text: I’ve been in your shoes before
annotations.comment: So they do get reassigned? If so, why wouldn’t the narrator at least think about starting over with a new person?

paragraphs.body: “What do you mean the details don’t matter? I want to know what happened. ”
Maybe X wasn’t who I thought he was.

“I was jealous once, like you, and thought I could change things.” he replies between slow breaths, “ So I tried to change it. I was so sure giving Sophia a hug would make her feel better… ya know, prove to her that I was real and that I was worth keeping around.”
“So, you broke the rules?”

“No. Right when I was about to do it, I heard the voice of someone from headquarters in my head telling me not to… to remember the consequences. I hadn’t heard a voice from headquarters so clearly since I was matched with Sophia.”

annotations.comment: I love this detail. I do think you should consider making X more of a character, not just someone who shows up to give a piece of advice and then leave.

paragraphs.body: “ So that’s it? You just gave up?”
“ I was reasonable. I didn’t want to risk Sophia’s life. You should be too.”
X is exactly who I thought he was. Weak. Scared. Not my friend.
I turn my attention away from X as he fades back into Sophia’s room and focus on Marly.
I know exactly what I need to do: Initiate Phase Two

As I focus back in on the girls, they huddle around each other, applying the same mauve lipliner to each of their lips. As Marly goes to reapply it to her own lips, I knock the pencil on the ground, just under the spot Becca’s heel lands, breaking the pencil in half.
“Shit. I just broke the lip liner. Mar, girl, how’d you even drop it?”
“ I don’t know. It just, like, flew out of my hand. I swear I was literally just holding it.”
“You guys are so drunk,” Sophia replies, “The uber will be here in six minutes, so get your stuff together.”

As Marly and Becca shove their belongings into their comically small purses, I can’t help but focus on how good it felt to be that close to Marly. To have our hands touch the same object.  I’ve never been this close to her, and I need to get closer. I’m so close to getting her back, I know it.
I don’t care what X says, I’m going to get Marly back. Headquarters doesn’t scare me.

annotations.comment: There’s a lot to like here, Luna. I love the addition of X and learning about how the girls experience the IFs. I felt it was a bit rushed — you sort of showed us the same thing twice, with the narrator interacting with objects and the girls just responding to that. I felt just seeing that once was sufficient, unless the second time would include some heightening — going further, trying something more, having another effect. I also loved X but wanted more from him. Right now, he appears, gives good advice, and leaves — what can his character bring to the story? What role can he play? It’s a tricky piece and I can see you’re still figuring things out. Keep going! Keep making choices!
